observations: Flat, no-trade session — zero entries and zero open positions, so no overnight hold evaluation was needed. The scheduled 10:00 ET open-market run should handle entries once premarket populates the watchlist; if the watchlist is still empty at 10:00, the premarket trigger genuinely failed and should be re-run / investigated.

top_rejection_reason: relative volume below REL_VOL_MIN 1.5 (23/25); secondary: MACD histogram not rising and EMA-9 <= EMA-21 (consolidating, not breaking out)
notes: Watchlist was EMPTY at the 10:00 run (premarket 8:30 trigger never committed), so per the EOD/premarket guidance I manually re-ran scripts/premarket_scan.py at ~10:08 ET. It succeeded: VIX 16.39, SPY trending up, 25 sentiment-positive momentum names written (SNDK, TPL, MRNA, GNRC, GLW, TER, WDC, FIX, STX, CIEN, MPC, MCY, MBUU, AAPL, AMZN, MSFT, NVDA, META, AVGO, ORCL, LLY, JPM, XOM, PANW, V) and daily_context.json refreshed to today. Computed live indicators for all 25 and evaluated the full entry gate. NO symbol passed all criteria. Dominant blocker is rel_vol < 1.5: rel_vol = cumulative IEX 5-min volume / 20d avg daily volume, which reads low this early in the session and on the free IEX feed. Only MCY (2.55) and MBUU (4.0) cleared rel_vol but both failed trend/momentum (EMA9<EMA21, RSI low-30s/40s, MACD falling) — pullbacks, not breakouts, so correctly skipped. Account healthy (equity ~$100k, no PDT, no flags). Conservative correct outcome: zero forced entries. Account still bootstrap with 0 completed trades. NOTE for ops: the 8:30 ET premarket scan continues to not auto-fire — recurring zero-population pattern; schedule/trigger needs investigation.
